title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Major Metcalf helps Jiles Davis shovel snow from the back door of Monkswell Manor. The Major, a fitness enthusiast and battle-hardened veteran, eats an early breakfast prepared by Molly Davis. After breakfast, Mr. Paravicini arrives for a simple meal, behaving flamboyantly toward Molly. While Jiles works to clear paths to the boiler room and chicken coop, Molly is interrupted by a telephone call in the library.

Constable Higgins from the Berkshire Police Department informs Molly of an emergency. He states that Sergeant Trotter is being sent to the manor, despite Molly's warning that the roads are impassable due to snow. The caller, identified as Inspector Hoggart, abruptly hangs up after instructing Molly to follow the Sergeant's directions.

A frightened Molly informs Jiles of the police call. The couple becomes anxious, speculating that they might be in trouble for minor infractions, such as using ration coupons or bringing butter from Ireland. Their conversation is interrupted by Mrs. Poole, a tenant, who complains about the cold living room. Mrs. Poole and Mrs. Poynter then criticize the guests, specifically questioning the character of the architect Christopher Raun and the arrival of the "odd foreigner," Mr. Paravicini. Mr. Paravicini enters the room quietly and reveals that he has been eavesdropping on their conversation, noting that he never forgets what he overhears.
